Threat: Unauthorised public viewing of dashboard data
Vulnerability: Web security group allows public HTTP from 0.0.0.0/0
Event / Incident: Anyone on the internet can open the dashboard if they know the public IP
Consequence: Operational data may be exposed without access control
ISO 27001 / 27017 Control: A.9 Access control / A.13 Network security
Action / Initial Treatment: Restrict public exposure and add controlled access path
Evidence / Source: cfstack.yml lines 205 to 215; AWS security group screenshot needed

Threat: Unauthorised public viewing of dashboard data
Vulnerability: Dashboard displays city operations data
Event / Incident: Traffic and environmental readings can be viewed from the public web page
Consequence: Operational information may be disclosed or misused
ISO 27001 / 27017 Control: A.8 Asset management / A.13 Network security
Action / Initial Treatment: Review what data should be public and protect the dashboard
Evidence / Source: cfstack.yml lines 307 to 354; DBLoad.js lines 9 to 36

Vulnerability: Application renders personnel_data fields
Event / Incident: Employee records are shown in the web dashboard
Consequence: Personal and internal staff information may be exposed
ISO 27001 / 27017 Control: A.9 Access control / A.18 Compliance
Action / Initial Treatment: Remove or protect personnel data view

Vulnerability: Seed data includes names, contacts, and clearance levels
Event / Incident: The application can expose staff names, emails, roles, and clearance levels
Consequence: Privacy and reputational harm may occur
ISO 27001 / 27017 Control: A.8 Asset classification / A.18 Data protection
Action / Initial Treatment: Classify data and limit displayed fields

Vulnerability: PHP display_errors is enabled
Event / Incident: Runtime errors may reveal application paths or details
Consequence: Attackers may gain useful technical information
ISO 27001 / 27017 Control: A.12 Operations security
Action / Initial Treatment: Disable public error display and log errors privately

Vulnerability: Connection exceptions are printed to the web page
Event / Incident: Database connection failure details may be shown to users
Consequence: Connection details and stack behaviour may be exposed
ISO 27001 / 27017 Control: A.12 Operations security / A.14 Secure development
Action / Initial Treatment: Use generic user errors and private logs

Vulnerability: PHP app connects to MongoDB using the Mongo EC2 public IP
Event / Incident: The web tier reaches the database through a public route
Consequence: Database traffic path is exposed and hard to control
ISO 27001 / 27017 Control: A.13 Network security / ISO 27017 cloud network controls
Action / Initial Treatment: Use private subnet access and security group source rules

Vulnerability: MongoDB URI uses mongodb:// with no TLS option shown
Event / Incident: Database traffic may not be protected in transit
Consequence: Data or credentials may be exposed on the path
ISO 27001 / 27017 Control: A.10 Cryptography / A.13 Network security
Action / Initial Treatment: Use TLS for database connections

Vulnerability: MongoDB port 27017 is open to 0.0.0.0/0
Event / Incident: Anyone on the internet can attempt a MongoDB connection
Consequence: Database compromise or data disclosure may occur
ISO 27001 / 27017 Control: A.9 Access control / A.13 Network security
Action / Initial Treatment: Allow MongoDB only from the webserver security group
Evidence / Source: cfstack.yml lines 154 to 164; AWS security group screenshot needed

Vulnerability: MongoDB listens on 0.0.0.0
Event / Incident: MongoDB accepts connections on all interfaces
Consequence: Database service is exposed beyond the intended local host
ISO 27001 / 27017 Control: A.13 Network security / A.12 Secure configuration
Action / Initial Treatment: Bind MongoDB to a private interface

Vulnerability: MongoDB EC2 is deployed in PublicSubnet1
Event / Incident: Database is placed in the public tier
Consequence: Database is easier to scan and attack
ISO 27001 / 27017 Control: A.13 Network security / ISO 27017 shared responsibility
Action / Initial Treatment: Move database to a private subnet

Vulnerability: Stack outputs the MongoDB server public IP
Event / Incident: Database public IP is exposed as deployment output
Consequence: The group may rely on direct public database access
ISO 27001 / 27017 Control: A.9 Access control / A.13 Network security
Action / Initial Treatment: Remove public DB output after redesign

Vulnerability: Database seed includes personnel data
Event / Incident: A database breach exposes employee records
Consequence: Personal data and internal clearance information may be exposed
ISO 27001 / 27017 Control: A.8 Asset classification / A.18 Compliance
Action / Initial Treatment: Restrict database access and classify personnel data

Vulnerability: Database seed includes operational logs
Event / Incident: A database breach exposes operational events
Consequence: Operational behaviour may be disclosed
ISO 27001 / 27017 Control: A.8 Asset management / A.12 Logging
Action / Initial Treatment: Limit database access and review data sensitivity

Vulnerability: DBLoad.js drops collections before loading seed data
Event / Incident: Running the script at the wrong time may remove existing data
Consequence: Application data may be lost during loading or testing
ISO 27001 / 27017 Control: A.12 Change control / A.17 Continuity
Action / Initial Treatment: Use controlled load scripts and backups

Vulnerability: Public subnets assign public IPs on launch
Event / Incident: Instances in public subnets can receive public addresses
Consequence: Resources may be reachable from the internet
ISO 27001 / 27017 Control: A.13 Network security
Action / Initial Treatment: Place only required public services in public subnets

Vulnerability: Public route table sends 0.0.0.0/0 to the internet gateway
Event / Incident: Public subnet traffic can route directly to the internet
Consequence: Attack surface increases for public resources
ISO 27001 / 27017 Control: A.13 Network security
Action / Initial Treatment: Separate public and private route tables by tier

Threat: Weak separation between public and private tiers
Vulnerability: Private subnets exist but MongoDB is placed in a public subnet
Event / Incident: The database is not using the private tier
Consequence: Internal data service is exposed more than needed
ISO 27001 / 27017 Control: A.13 Network security / ISO 27017 cloud controls
Action / Initial Treatment: Move MongoDB into private subnet and restrict routes

Vulnerability: Database security group allows access from any internet address
Event / Incident: Database access is not limited to the webserver tier
Consequence: Database may be scanned or attacked directly
ISO 27001 / 27017 Control: A.9 Access control / A.13 Network security
Action / Initial Treatment: Use least privilege source security group rules

Vulnerability: Template description states the environment is deliberately insecure
Event / Incident: The deployment starts from a known insecure state
Consequence: If left unchanged, known weaknesses remain in AWS
ISO 27001 / 27017 Control: A.12 Change control / A.14 Secure development
Action / Initial Treatment: Track baseline weaknesses and secure them in later phase

Vulnerability: Template creates open public security group rules
Event / Incident: Open rules are reproduced every time the stack is deployed
Consequence: Misconfiguration may persist through redeployment
ISO 27001 / 27017 Control: A.12 Operations security / A.13 Network security
Action / Initial Treatment: Update template rather than only changing console settings

Vulnerability: Seed script clears collections before inserting data
Event / Incident: A rerun may delete existing test data
Consequence: Loss of stored application data during testing
ISO 27001 / 27017 Control: A.12 Change control / A.17 Continuity
Action / Initial Treatment: Add operator warning and backup step before reset

Vulnerability: RDP port 3389 is open to 0.0.0.0/0 when GroupSize is 3
Event / Incident: A Windows instance may expose RDP to the internet
Consequence: Remote compromise of management host may occur
ISO 27001 / 27017 Control: A.9 Access control / A.13 Network security
Action / Initial Treatment: Restrict RDP or use managed remote access

Vulnerability: The VDI is placed in PublicSubnet1 if deployed
Event / Incident: The management host is placed in the public network tier
Consequence: Management access may become directly exposed
ISO 27001 / 27017 Control: A.13 Network security
Action / Initial Treatment: Place management host behind controlled access
